# research тАФ plugin conventions
> Context for any agent (or human) editing **this plugin itself**.
> For executing one of the skills, read its `SKILL.md` тАФ each is self-contained.

## What this plugin is

A collection of skills for **reading external sources with intent** тАФ understanding what arguments claim, what evidence they produce, and what remains open. The through-line is **argument anatomy**: every document making a claim can be dissected into Gap / Claim / Mechanism / Evidence / Conclusion, and every dissection can be compared against your own claim to locate prior art and open ground.

Four skills today: `dissect` (single or batch argument anatomy тАФ one note per document; forensic mode also audits how the user already cites the document), `untangle` (the relational structure across N sources тАФ lineage / clusters / contradictions / contested ground, with optional claim-framed positioning), `critique` (adversarial assessment of one paper into a referee report тАФ claimтЖФevidence audit + self-attack), and `provenance` (self-audit of the user's own documents тАФ trace every load-bearing citation back to a verified source, classify first-hand / second-hand / orphan, emit a quarantine list). The verbs sit on one spine: `dissect` gives the **nodes** (one argument's anatomy), `untangle` gives the **edges** (how arguments relate), `critique` **assesses** whether one argument's evidence holds, `provenance` **audits your use** of all of them тАФ the reflexive corner (critique's mirror: their claims vs their evidence тЖФ your citations vs your sources). `untangle`, `critique`, and `provenance` all consume `dissect` (untangle reuses skeletons to find edges; critique grounds deeper on attack surfaces; provenance dispatches forensic dissects as its repair arm). Candidate skill pending: `feedback` (extract design implications from competitor empirical results). See `docs/adr/027-research-plugin.md` for the family charter; `030` (critique), `031` (the mapтЖТuntangle rename + relational reframe), and `042` (provenance тАФ the self-audit verb).

**Domain-agnostic by design.** The GapтЖТClaimтЖТMechanismтЖТEvidence framework applies to academic papers, technical blog posts, competitor analyses, RFCs, and design proposals. The `dissect` skill's output is always the same skeleton тАФ making cross-document comparison possible regardless of source type.

This plugin lives inside the `skills` marketplace (`ChenPaulYu/skills`). It is **independent** тАФ no dependency on `nav` or `shape`. Future skills that involve file-writing conventions may introduce soft cross-plugin recommendations (one-way, guarded, same pattern as nav тЖТ shape).

## The research through-line

The unit of analysis is the **argument**, not the document.

Any well-formed argument can be decomposed into:
1. **Gap** тАФ what is missing or broken in the world? Why is it a problem?
2. **Claim** тАФ what does this work propose to fix it?
3. **Mechanism** тАФ how does it technically work?
4. **Evidence** тАФ what experiments / results support the claim?
5. **Conclusion** тАФ what does the author want the reader to believe?

This decomposition serves two uses simultaneously:
- **Understanding**: what does this work actually argue?
- **Positioning**: what does this work leave open? Where does my claim live relative to theirs?

The second use is what makes literature review productive. You are not cataloguing papers тАФ you are mapping what has been argued and proven so you can locate what hasn't been.

## Conventions for skills inside this plugin

> Repo-wide **authoring + maintenance** rules (naming, skills-root-relative paths, stack-neutral examples, frontmatter `description`, ADR-on-new-skill, the site-map gate, versioning) live in the repo-root [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md). research-specific:

- **Read-only by default**: `dissect` surfaces a dissection note and asks where to save it. It never writes without explicit user confirmation.
- **Output format is the artifact**: a dissection note is a structured markdown file comparable across documents. The format is fixed so multiple notes can be laid side by side.
- **Claim injection is optional but recommended**: if the user's own claim is available, inject it so the skill can produce an "Implications for [your claim]" section. Without it, the skill still produces the structural skeleton; the implications section is blank.

## Where things live

```
.claude-plugin/plugin.json   тЖТ research's manifest (the version + metadata owner)
CLAUDE.md                    тЖТ тЖР you are here (research-specific)
skills/<name>/SKILL.md       тЖТ individual skills, each self-contained
```

Repo-wide layout + ADRs live in the repo-root [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md), which also holds all editing rules (new-skill тЖТ ADR, the тШЕ authoring checks, renaming + versioning, the site-map gate). research adds nothing beyond the marketplace defaults.


---

# shape тАФ plugin conventions
> Editing this plugin. To run a skill, read its `SKILL.md` (self-contained).

## What it is

**Forward motion: give intent its form, all the way to running, verified code.** Sibling to `nav` тАФ the two split the lifecycle cleanly: **shape pushes the work forward** (converge тЖТ plan тЖТ build), **nav keeps the result healthy** (audit тЖТ refactor тЖТ map). Domain = the loop **converge a decision тЖТ record it legibly тЖТ keep it current тЖТ build it real**.

> Identity note (ADR-011): shape was originally scoped "pre-build". Adding `build` deliberately widened it to forward-motion-through-execution тАФ the natural terminus of the spine, a real instance, not scope-creep. nav stays the maintenance half.

## Spine (through-line)

**Converge by a real, disposable instance тАФ never a description.** The instance's *form* varies: a verbal grounded fork (elicit), or a rendered interactive artifact (mockup). Its standing form is the blueprints board тАФ **one current state, two renders** (agent reads markdown, human reads an HTML projection; deps point downstream only). Its **terminus** is `build` тАФ the disposable instances converge the decision; build makes it the durable real thing and verifies it against the mockup (the running system is the third render).

**Progressive disclosure runs through both renders.** Every artifact leads with its interface and drills in only as needed тАФ the agent's render of that is markdown structure (a `head -12`-able header + TL;DR, sections that lead with their point), the human's is the click-to-reveal board. Same interface-first principle as `nav`'s file headers; two media.

**The verb is the only door for its deliverable (ADR-038).** If you are about to hand-produce something a verb encodes тАФ a comparison mockup (`shape-mockup`), a converge volley (`shape-elicit`), a decided behaviour-changing change (`nav-do`) тАФ **fire the verb instead of hand-rolling the artifact**. The artifact is not the deliverable; the *protocol around it* is (post-pick offers, check bracket, gated diffs) тАФ hand-rolling produces the artifact and silently drops the protocol. Having done the craft ambiently many times is the *risk signal*, not a waiver: fluency is exactly when the door gets skipped (two sightings: 2026-06-03 execution, 2026-06-11 converge). This does not make verbs auto-fire тАФ it governs the case where you're *already producing* the deliverable.

**Canon is single-writer and freeze-gated (ADR-041).** A project's core docs (`docs/core/*`-class тАФ its constitution) are written by exactly one door: an explicitly-summoned `shape-position`, and even then only at freeze moments (campaign close ┬╖ explicit per-item freeze order ┬╖ root-doc birth) тАФ mid-campaign rulings land in a campaign log, because **ruling тЙа ratification**. Every other verb and all ambient conversation: never write core тАФ append a one-line pending amendment to `docs/core/amendments.md` (what changed ┬╖ evidence ┬╖ by which verb); position adjudicates the ledger as each summon's first feeding, and reconcile sweeps its leftovers. Freshness-law carve-out: stale header = fix same-commit; **stale canon = ledger entry (tracked debt), never a direct edit**. This is ADR-038's clause at the canon layer. Full protocol: `plugins/shape/skills/position/references/write-protocol.md`.

**Below 90% тЖТ ask (core principle, shared with `nav` rule тСж).** When unsure about scope, boundaries, or intent, stop and ask тАФ never guess. This is the spine of the whole family, not a per-skill add-on: `elicit` *is* this rule as a workflow (it converges by asking, not answering); `mockup` lets the user point; `align` decides *with* the user; `reconcile` gates every destructive step per-file. Any autonomous driver built on top runs **only while confidence holds**. The moment scope/boundary/intent drops below 90%, it stops and asks rather than plowing ahead тАФ the cost of pausing to clarify is far smaller than unwinding a wrong guess.

## Members

Grouped by verb (mirrors `nav`'s family shape):

- **converge** тАФ turn an open question into a decision:
  - `mockup` тАФ render-to-decide: a real interactive HTML (UI mockup, or diagram for backend/agent/data/flow). *(built)*
  - `elicit` тАФ draw the decision out by a grounded fork (react-not-author, drill-to-principle, compress-to-one-line; weight-adaptive exit тАФ stop on the snap; summoned, not auto) тЖТ a `thoughts/` doc. *(built)*
  - `dogfood` тАФ use a *built* feature that feels unsmooth for real: drive the REAL interface (`agent-browser` / `curl` / CLI) against a list of user intents, report **friction** (works but clunky тЖТ a UX idea) + the **coverage gaps** that fall out (an intent with no path тЖТ a logic hole). Experience-first; logic-coverage is the byproduct. Output is a friction report (not a mandatory mockup); render is an optional redesign hand-off. A gap's layer (missing intent = direction ┬╖ dead-end = incomplete) routes it, handed to `elicit`. Borrows `/verify`┬╖`/run`'s drive-the-real-app method (asks "is it smooth + what's missing", not "is it correct"). *(built; ADR-033, supersedes ADR-020)*
  - `position` тАФ **author the canon layer**: a gated multi-feeding *campaign* that lands core (principle-wise) docs. Five kernels: **ingest-assess** (per-feeding delta report тАФ new / metabolized / conflict / needs-ruling тАФ with *guarding*: conflicts with locked decisions block, not absorb) ┬╖ **altitude instrument** (axiom/principle/approach/bet + the *churn alarm*: a second flip on the same decision = form-layer debate, lift to the rule) ┬╖ **two-tier landing** (`core/` = user-ratified + self-contained; `thoughts/` = dated hypotheses) ┬╖ **graduation** (core *grows* by user-frozen thoughts, never scaffolded) ┬╖ **periodic re-audit** (canon accretes mid-campaign; pre-landing `frame-first-principles` self-audit). **Doc-class framed** тАФ any core doc, not just product positioning. **Write protocol (ADR-041)**: core single-writer + freeze-gated тАФ mid-campaign rulings тЖТ the campaign log; other verbs тЖТ the `docs/core/amendments.md` ledger, adjudicated as each summon's first feeding. Mirror of `reconcile`: position *births* canon (pre-code, authority-driven), reconcile *keeps it true* (post-code, reality-driven). A meta-skill (ADR-008): borrows elicit / mockup / first-principles by protocol. *(built; ADR-035, write protocol ADR-041)*
- **project** тАФ render the current plan:
  - `align` тАФ read `thoughts/` + real state тЖТ decide now/next/later *with the user* тЖТ write `plan.md` (agent) + regenerate `overview.html` (human). The pre-build mirror of `nav-sync`'s codebase map. *(built)*
- **reconcile** тАФ keep the archive honest:
  - `reconcile` тАФ judge which `thoughts/`, `plans/` **and `mockups/`** entries have drifted (code/headers ┬╖ self-declaration ┬╖ date) тЖТ **amend** a stale *fact* in place, **prune/consolidate** a wholly-stale doc, or **retire** a shipped decision's mockup folder (salvage residue тЖТ prune, git as deep archive; parked decisions + stamped structural locks stay), *with the user*, write-gated. Amend syncs only what built code shows; a *decision*-change is out of scope тЖТ it recommends `shape-elicit` and says so (the active form of "no new decisions during render"). Also sweeps the **`docs/core/amendments.md` ledger** (absorbed/reversed entries тЖТ prune тАФ the ledger's exit) and flags **canon-grade** thoughts (settled axiom/principle тЖТ recommend `shape-position`, never write core; ADR-041). The pre-build mirror of `nav-audit` + careful `nav-refactor`. *(built; amend in ADR-014; maintains `plans/` per ADR-017; sweeps `mockups/` per ADR-037 тАФ the enforcement point of mockup's retire-on-ship; ledger + canon-grade per ADR-041)*
- **build** тАФ make it real:
  - `setup` тАФ **scaffold a new project to a VERIFIED running baseline** on a three-layer knowledge base: neutral engine(archetype тЖТ principles тЖТ reference тЖТ preflight тЖТ scaffold тЖТ verification chain тЖТ staged commits тЖТ write-back)┬╖ `references/stack-principles.md`(the user's per-concern rulings тАФ PythonтЖТuv, frontendтЖТReact+pnpmтАж + docs/git/.gitignore conventions + port registry; fork-with-your-own)┬╖ `references/archetypes/<name>.md`(composition + glue + field gotchas + the chain + named living **exemplars** тАФ never prose-embedded file bodies). **Done = chain green, not files written**(web: dev script for real тЖТ proxy curl тЖТ browser click + screenshot тЖТ teardown). New archetype тЖТ exemplar mode + setup log тЖТ graduates on the user's nod(the цЩЙхНЗ loop, 4th appearance). Distinct from the "no init" razor below: that rejects mkdir-theatre; setup's payload is the structure default scaffolding skips. *(built; ADR-036)*
  - `build` тАФ drive the plan's In-progress column to done: per item, ground via `nav-plan` тЖТ implement with `nav-refactor` discipline + injectтЖФcheck тЖТ verify (browser screenshot vs the item's mockup; or test-gate) тЖТ move to Shipped тЖТ `shape-align`. Autonomous but **confidence-gated** (stop below 90%). A meta-skill (orchestrates siblings, never re-implements/calls them). The cross-plugin orchestrator. **Parallel dispatch = a scheduling policy with a proposal gate, never a mode (ADR-040)** тАФ *workflow is build's muscle, never its brain*: ground ALL items upfront (read-only fan-out; each plan's Critical files + Verification are the scheduling inputs) тЖТ three criteria (disjoint incl. test-import paths ┬╖ тЙе90% decided ┬╖ no shared-primitive risk) тЖТ propose "conflicting serial prefix + disjoint parallel tail"; **the user's nod starts the batch** (= the multi-agent opt-in) тЖТ shared tree (no worktrees, decided trade), in-batch agents write-don't-test, `done|blocked` тЖТ join on ONE authoritative gate, check brackets serial. Execution parallelizes; adjudication never does. *(built; parallel policy ADR-040)*
- **`blueprints/` = convention**, not a skill тАФ the artifact container the verbs read/write. Layout + `plan.md` shape + `overview.html` contract + the two-renders pipeline live in [`skills/align/references/blueprints-spec.md`](plugins/shape/skills/align/references/blueprints-spec.md).
- **`doctor` = deferred** тАФ an orchestrator (elicit тЖТ align тЖТ reconcile) only crystallizes once there's genuinely enough to orchestrate. nav's own `doctor` was later **retired** (ADR-021) for being too thin a sequence (`audit тЖТ sync`, 2 steps) тАФ a caution that an orchestrator with little to orchestrate is redundant ceremony, not a convenience. Don't build shape's until the sequence earns it.
- ~~`position` = candidate (planned, raw)~~ тЖТ **crystallized 2026-06-10** under the converge group above (same-day field validation: two canon docs from one engine, first live graduation, user-ordered reframe to doc-class) тАФ [ADR-035](docs/adr/035-position-canon-authoring-verb.md); founding evidence in [`docs/observations/2026-06-10-position-verb-canon-campaign.md`](docs/observations/2026-06-10-position-verb-canon-campaign.md).

There is deliberately **no `init` skill** тАФ scaffolding the `blueprints/` tree folds into `align`'s first run (ask location once, create idempotently). A skill for `mkdir -p` would be structure-theatre. *(`setup` does not violate this razor: its payload is archetype determination + standing principles + gotchas + an enforced verification chain тАФ the structure default scaffolding skips, not the mkdir. See ADR-036.)*

## The seams with `nav` (hard deps one-way; soft recommendations either way)

shape is the forward-motion half; `nav` is the maintenance half. The dependency rule has two tiers:
- **Hard deps (import / call / breaks-without-it) are one-way: shape тЖТ nav.** `nav` must run fully standalone тАФ never *depend* on shape.
- **Soft recommendations (a guarded *offer* / hand-off that degrades if absent) may go nav тЖТ shape.** These already exist and are fine: `nav-plan` Stage 2 hands visual ambiguity to `shape-mockup`, and Stage 4 *offers* a `shape-mockup` summary (guarded тАФ omitted if shape isn't installed). See ADR-012.

Record the seams; don't blur them:

1. **`blueprints/` is the hand-off artifact to `nav-plan` тАФ both directions.** shape converges intent into blueprints; `nav-plan` grounds a thought/spec into a code-level implementation plan **and writes it back into `blueprints/plans/`** when the tree is present (soft `nav тЖТ shape` preference, ADR-017). `align` triages forward; `nav-plan` grounds one item down тАФ adjacent verbs, not overlapping. The grounded plan stays co-located so the whole arc (decision тЖТ status тЖТ grounded-how) lives in one tree.
2. **`reconcile` consumes the file headers `nav-sync` maintains, and maintains `plans/` + `mockups/`.** `head -12` file headers make "is this implemented?" answerable cheaply тАФ the strongest staleness signal. reconcile keeps **`thoughts/`, `plans/`, and `mockups/`** current (a plan whose steps all shipped is stale like an implemented thought; a shipped decision's mockup retires once its residue is absorbed тАФ ADR-037).
3. **`build` is the concentrated seam.** It *controls* the loop (lives in shape, forward-motion) while *calling* nav's code-side protocols per item: `nav-plan` (ground) тЖТ `nav-refactor` discipline (implement) тЖТ `nav-sync` (cheap header grounding for inject), then writes back via `shape-align`. Direction stays one-way: shape тЖТ nav.

## Conventions for skills inside this plugin

> Repo-wide **authoring + maintenance** rules (naming, skills-root-relative paths, stack-neutral examples, frontmatter `description`, ADR-on-new-skill, the site-map gate, versioning) live in the repo-root [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md). Shape-specific:

- **тШЕ Progressive disclosure in every produced artifact** (so an agent scans fast, not just the human): a `thoughts/` doc opens with `# <title> тАФ <one-line role>` + a тЙд3-line TL;DR/status blockquote (`head -12` yields the gist without reading the body), sections lead with their point, enumerations go in tables, key terms bolded; a `mockup` artifact opens with a top-comment stating what it is ┬╖ the candidates ┬╖ the pick. This is `nav`'s interface-first (rule тСб) applied to shape's outputs. Every skill that emits a doc/artifact (`elicit`, `mockup`, `align`) enforces it.
- **Write-gated**: `align` and `reconcile` write files тАФ show what will be written (or a diff) before committing. `reconcile`'s destructive ops follow the safety rules in its `SKILL.md` (check tracked/untracked, never `mv`-then-`rm`, diff before delete).
- **Skills don't invoke each other**: they reference sibling protocols by name (e.g. reconcile тЖТ "run `shape-align`"), never re-implement or call them.
- **Cost tier (ADR-058/059)**: the *criterion* (mechanical sweep/format/render тЖТ `model: sonnet` frontmatter, turn-level) is owned by the repo-root [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md); shape's tiered verbs are **`reconcile`** and **`align`**. Judgment-heavy verbs (`elicit`, `position`, `build`'s adjudication) stay on the session model. The same tier powers the browser-verify slot's `browser-verifier` subagent (below).

## browser-verify тАФ a shared per-project capability slot

Three skills need to *see* the running thing тАФ `mockup` (render the artifact), `align` (open the board), `build` (verify each item against its mockup). They share **one** pluggable capability, not three copies (N+1 trigger):

- **Default implementation: `agent-browser`** (vercel-labs/agent-browser) тАФ a browser-automation CLI. Detect: `which agent-browser`. Usage: `agent-browser open <url>` тЖТ `agent-browser screenshot <file>.png`; `snapshot -i` / `click @e` / `fill @e "..."` to interact.
- **Don't hardcode the tool тАФ name the capability.** A skill describes the contract ("open the running feature тЖТ screenshot тЖТ compare to mockup тЖТ **close when done**"); `agent-browser` is the named **default**, not the contract. The close is *part of* the contract, not optional cleanup: an unclosed headless browser silently burns a core after the pass returns, and the cost lands on the user's machine тАФ invisible from inside the loop, so it has to be in the contract, not left to judgment (trap #3). Caveat: this written contract is *polled* knowledge, not a *cue* тАФ it fires only if the executing path actually walks it. So bind the close where open *executes*: write each verify step so its last move is `close` (a walked step is a cue, a reference doc is not), and lean on a turn-end browser reaper as the cross-path backstop. The doc raises the odds it's in-context; it does not make it fire.
- **If missing, fail helpfully тАФ never silently skip.** Surface a 3-way confidence-gate choice: **(a) install** [recommended] тАФ `npm install -g agent-browser` (or `brew`/`cargo install agent-browser`) then `agent-browser install`; or as a skill `npx skills add vercel-labs/agent-browser`. **(b)** proceed without visual verify this run (flag items to eyeball). **(c)** skip verify for this item. Report what was skipped (no silent caps).
- **Per-project override:** a project may bind a different helper (Playwright, puppeteer) in its own CLAUDE.md; the agent reads that. Absent an override, the default is agent-browser.
- **Skills don't call skills:** name the capability + describe the contract; the executing agent invokes the tool (CLI shell-out, or triggers the agent-browser skill in its own turn).
- **Delegate the pass to the `browser-verifier` subagent тАФ the cost tier (ADR-058).** The slot's default *executor* is the plugin's [`agents/browser-verifier.md`](plugins/shape/agents/browser-verifier.md) (model: sonnet). The caller injects URL ┬╖ screenshot destination ┬╖ expectation (mockup path or stated behaviour) ┬╖ any interaction steps / tool override, and receives back a compact verdict (`PASS | DRIFT | BLOCKED | MISSING-TOOL` + reason + paths) тАФ the drive-and-capture loop and its **image tokens stay in the subagent's context**, never entering the main conversation (where every screenshot is re-paid on every later turn). Delegate whenever the pass is mechanical (build's per-item verify, mockup's capture confirmation, align's board check); drive inline only when the user is being walked through the page live. The close-contract binds inside the subagent тАФ its last move is `close`. Ships with the plugin, so it works on any machine that installed shape.
- **Verify economy тАФ capture at decision points, not per step.** A screenshot is *evidence* (Shipped-evidence, friction-evidence, a pick's record), never a progress note: `build` captures once per item at land (mid-item verification is the test gate); `dogfood` screenshots at friction/gap moments and records video **only on explicit request**; `mockup` confirms a render once, not per iteration; `align` opens the board without capturing. Screenshots live on disk (report/mockups tree) тАФ referenced by path, not pasted into the chat.
- **Known traps:** the slot's field manual ([`references/browser-verify-gotchas.md`](plugins/shape/references/browser-verify-gotchas.md)) is organized by scope тАФ **universal** (session isolation ┬╖ assert state-not-text ┬╖ measure-before-toggle ┬╖ selector-over-pixel clicks ┬╖ poll-don't-snapshot ┬╖ rule-out-mock-data ┬╖ daemon-reset ┬╖ teardown/`close` with its knowledgeтЙаtrigger cue-ladder + provenance-keyed reaper), **audio** (dual-backend mute ┬╖ real-input for user-activation), **transform-canvas** (`position:fixed` тЖТ portal to body), and **React+Vite** (controlled-input native-setter, HMR console ghosts). Read before trusting a screenshot or console scan, and `close` the browser when the pass ends.

## Status

Eight skills built (`mockup`, `elicit`, `dogfood`, `position`, `setup`, `align`, `reconcile`, `build`); `doctor` (orchestrator) deferred until the family proves out. `elicit`'s behavioral spec comes from `docs/observations/2026-05-29-thought-mode-how-paul-converges.md`; `build` + the browser-verify slot land in ADR-011; `position` lands in ADR-035; `setup` in ADR-036. History: `docs/observations/2026-05-29-shape-plugin.md`.
